title: ELF: Embedded Language Flows
core_claim: ELF proposes a continuous embedding-space diffusion/flow-matching language model that mostly stays continuous until the final token mapping, and reports better generation quality with fewer sampling steps than leading discrete and continuous DLMs.
triage_rationale: The paper targets an active and technically plausible direction in language modeling and claims a concrete modeling change with experimental gains, which makes it worth review. The topic is also likely interesting for podcast discussion because it sits at the intersection of diffusion, flow matching, and language generation.
likely_red_flags: The abstract makes broad performance claims without providing task details, dataset scope, or evaluation settings.
This is a recent arXiv submission with no source signals beyond authorship and categories, so claims should be treated as preliminary.
